meaning: n.(液体等的)喷射,冲刺vt.(液体等) 喷射vi.(液体等) 喷出,迸发,冲刺
examples: I saw flames spurt from the roof.
A spurt of diesel came from one valve and none from the other.
The average boy of 14 years old is only beginning his adolescent growth spurt.
The first big growth spurt took place in the 1960s behind high tariff walls.
Indiana followed that with a 9-4 spurt to take a 45-37 lead and never trailed again.
